name: python3-rpyc
title: python3-rpyc
summary: Python 3 library for remote procedure calls
description:
Python 3 library for remote procedure calls
RPyC (pronounced as are-pie-see), or Remote Python Call, is a transparent
Python library for symmetrical remote procedure calls, clustering and
distributed-computing. RPyC makes use of object-proxying, a technique that
employs Python’s dynamic nature, to overcome the physical boundaries between
processes and computers, so that remote objects can be manipulated as if they
were local.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use rpyc_classic because Python 3 library for remote procedure calls.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use rpyc_classic as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `rpyc_classic [options]` as documented upstream for rpyc_classic.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair rpyc_classic with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `rpyc_classic --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
